content:
# Project Status

## Knowledge Base

### Proof Patterns (reusable across targets)
- **Presheaf‚Üîsheaf iso bridge**: when a Mathlib iso criterion is stated about the
  *sheaf*-hom (e.g. `TopCat.Presheaf.isIso_iff_stalkFunctor_map_iso`, which is about
  `‚ü®‚Ä¶‚ü© : TopCat.Sheaf ‚Ä¶`), bundle `M.presheaf`/`N.presheaf` (sheaves via `M.isSheaf`)
  into `TopCat.Sheaf` and bridge presheaf-iso‚Üîsheaf-iso with
  `(fullyFaithfulSheafToPresheaf _ _).isIso_of_isIso_map` + `inferInstance`. Do NOT route
  via `TopCat.Sheaf.forget.ReflectsIsomorphisms` ‚Äî that instance will NOT synthesize
  through the coerced `PresheafedSpace`, even at `maxSynthPendingDepth 16`.
- **Additivity through `def` layers won't synth**: a `Functor.Additive` instance does not
  propagate through wrapping `def`s during synthesis. Two fixes that worked: (a)
  `inferInstanceAs` on the defeq underlying composition (e.g.
  `inferInstanceAs (SheafOfModules.forget _ ‚ãô PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).Additive`);
  (b) prove the `map_add` field explicitly: `change ‚Ä¶app eq; rw [Functor.map_add]; rfl`
  (NatTrans add is pointwise ‚Üí `rfl` closes). (a) is fragile to Mathlib redefining the def.
- **Right-derived foundation pattern (TWO-TIER as of iter-006)**:
  - *Heavy* `R‚Å±f_* := (pushforward f).rightDerived n` (relative pushforward, in `X.Modules`)
    still needs `[HasInjectiveResolutions X.Modules]` ‚Äî take it as an honest HYPOTHESIS until
    the heavy Grothendieck bridge lands (self-discharges, no rework).
  - *Light* scalar `H‚Å±(X,‚Ñ±)` is now **UNCONDITIONAL**: compute it in `TopCat.Sheaf Ab X`
    (an honest `Sheaf J A` where `Sheaf.isGrothendieckAbelian_of_essentiallySmall` APPLIES),
    via `Scheme.Modules.toAbSheaf : X.Modules ‚•§ TopCat.Sheaf Ab X` then
    `((absheafGlobalSections X).rightDerived i).obj ((toAbSheaf X).obj F)`. No hypothesis on
    `X.Modules`. (The OLD module-level `globalSectionsAb`/its `_additive` are now dead code ‚Äî
    HModule routes through the sheaf-level functor.)
- **Universe pinning is MANDATORY for the light bridge**: `TopCat.Sheaf C X` unfolds to
  `Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) C`, but unpinned `TopCat.Sheaf Ab X` leaves the size
  universe ambiguous and `Abelian`/`EnoughInjectives` then fail to synthesize **as terms**
  (they succeed under `by infer_instance` standalone ‚Äî misleading). Pin everything to `u`:
  `TopCat.Sheaf.{u} Ab.{u} X`, `IsGrothendieckAbelian.{u}`.
- **Category-level `HasInjectiveResolutions` has no Mathlib plural instance** from
  `EnoughInjectives`; build it as `‚ü®fun Z => InjectiveResolution.instHasInjectiveResolution Z‚ü©`
  (per-object). `IsGrothendieckAbelian.enoughInjectives` is a THEOREM (not instance) ‚Äî works as
  a term only with universes pinned.
- **HEAVY `X.Modules` Grothendieck bridge (LANDED iter-009, axiom-clean)** ‚Äî assemble in 5 steps:
  (1) `AB5OfSize (PresheafOfModules R‚ÇÄ)`: `HasExactColimitsOfShape K Ab` is NOT a registered
  instance (loops) ‚Äî extract via `AB5OfSize.ofShape (C := Ab) K`, lift to `C·µí·µñ ‚•§ Ab`, transport
  with `HasExactColimitsOfShape.domain_of_functor K (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf R‚ÇÄ)`. (2)
  `AB5OfSize (SheafOfModules R)`: take `Œ± = ùüô R.obj` (do NOT build a presheaf‚Üísheaf ring
  comparison); `PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (ùüô R.obj)` has FF right adjoint ‚Üí
  `adj.hasExactColimitsOfShape K`. (3) `HasSeparator (SheafOfModules R)`: sheafify the
  presheaf-level `PresheafOfModules.freeYoneda` separating family (Yoneda-representable ‚Üí local
  sections) via a left-adjoint-transport helper (faithful right adjoint), then `isSeparator_sigma`.
  (4) `IsGrothendieckAbelian (SheafOfModules R)` with empty `where` (4 fields synth) ‚Äî KEY ON THE
  `SheafOfModules` head (`Scheme.Modules` def-head is not unfolded by instance matching). (5)
  transfer to `X.Modules` via `inferInstanceAs (‚Ä¶ (SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf))` (defeq). Pin
  every universe to `u`.
- **`SheafOfModules R` separator = sheafified `freeYoneda`, NOT global free sheaves.** A separating
  family transports along a left adjoint with faithful right adjoint via `Gr.map_injective` +
  `ObjectProperty.ofObj_iff` + `Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_right` (mirrors
  `IsSeparating.of_equivalence`).
- **Symmetric-power functor on `ModuleCat` (LANDED iter-010, axiom-clean)** ‚Äî Mathlib has only the
  TYPE `SymmetricPower R Œπ M` (functorial action is a Mathlib TODO). Build the action in two layers:
  (L1) `SymmetricPower.map f := AddCon.lift ‚Ä¶ (SymmetricPower.mk ‚àò PiTensorProduct.map (fun _=>f)) ‚Ä¶`;
  well-definedness is `AddCon.addConGen_le` reduced to the `Rel.perm` generator, closed by
  `PiTensorProduct.map_tprod` + `SymmetricPower.tprod_equiv`; `map_smul'` via `AddCon.induction_on`;
  `map_id`/`map_comp` via `LinearMap.ext_on (span_tprod_eq_top ‚Ä¶)` + `map_tprod` (decomposables span).
  (L2) thin `ModuleCat` wrapper mirroring `ModuleCat.exteriorPower.functor`.
  **UNIVERSE TRAP (mandatory):** index `ModuleCat.symmetricPower` by `ULift.{u} (Fin n)`, NOT `Fin n`
  ‚Äî `SymmetricPower (R Œπ : Type u)` couples ring+index in one universe, so `Fin n : Type 0` forces
  `R : Type 0`. Functor signature `ModuleCat.{v} R ‚•§ ModuleCat.{max u v} R`.
- **Base-change `Sym^i`/`‚àß^i` over a ring hom ‚Äî instance-plumbing trap (iter-010, confirmed).** Both
  the symmetric (`found:presheafmod_sym_power`) and exterior (`found:exterior_power_base_change`)
  presheaf-level powers need a base-change map over the semilinear restriction `œÜ : R(U)‚ÜíR(V)`.
  `MultilinearMap.restrictScalars A` requires `[Module A N][IsScalarTower A B N]` (and same on the
  target `Sym[B] Œπ N`), which DO NOT synthesize from `[Algebra A B][Module B N]` alone (diamond-
  avoidance; named error `failed to synthesize Module A N`). Carry the A-module + `IsScalarTower` as
  EXPLICIT binders, or state the deliverable categorically over `restrictScalars œÜ` (carriers defeq,
  `rfl`). The two base-change lemmas are siblings; a shared `restrictScalars`-tower helper serves both.
- **Base-change power maps ‚Äî steps (1)‚Äì(4) LANDED axiom-clean (iter-012), BOTH flavours.** Recipe:
  (1) `Scheme.ringCatSheaf_commRing` = `@[reducible] noncomputable def` (NOT a global instance ‚Äî
  `Ring`-diamond vs the `RingCat`-level ring), carriers defeq so `inferInstanceAs (CommRing (X.presheaf.obj U))`;
  consume via `letI`. A class-typed `def` MUST carry `@[reducible]` (else "Definition of class type must
  be marked @[reducible]"). (2) linear-alg `baseChange` = restrict the fixed-base `ŒπMulti`/`tprod` to `A`
  (`MultilinearMap.restrictScalars A`; `AlternatingMap.restrictScalars` does NOT exist ‚Äî go through the
  multilinear form + `map_eq_zero_of_eq`), then the over-`A` universal property (`alternatingMapLinearEquiv`
  / `AddCon.lift`). (3) categorical `baseChangeMap : ‚ãÄ[R]^n M ‚Üí‚Çõ‚Çó[œÜ] ‚ãÄ[S]^n N` = build R-structures from œÜ
  (`letI Algebra R S := œÜ.toAlgebra`, `Module R N := Module.compHom N œÜ`, `IsScalarTower ‚Ä¶`), compose
  `baseChange ‚àò map gR`, repackage as `‚Üí‚Çõ‚Çó[œÜ]`. TWO traps: **NAME-CLASH** ‚Äî inside
  `namespace ModuleCat.exteriorPower`, `exteriorPower.map` binds the *categorical* map (wrong arg order);
  write `_root_.exteriorPower.map`. **`change` metavar** ‚Äî a `change` on a generator leaves the base ring
  a metavariable so `IsScalarTower` gets stuck; pin `baseChange (A:=R)(B:=S)(Œπ:=Œπ)(N:=N)`. Also pin
  `{R S : Type u} [CommRing R] [CommRing S]` explicit or `NonAssocSemiring R` fails to synth. See memory
  `lean-basechange-techniques`. REMAINING (steps 5/6): `baseChangeMap_id`/`_comp` (in the
  `restrictScalars{Id',Comp'}` form) + `PresheafOfModules.mk` assembly ‚Äî co-build in one iter.
- **Openwise + sheafified power functors ‚Äî steps (5)/(6) LANDED axiom-clean (iter-013), BOTH flavours
  ‚Äî Foundations power-functor substrate now COMPLETE.** (5a) naturality
  `ModuleCat.{ext,sym}.baseChangeMap_{id,comp}` ‚Äî generalize the composite to `œá:R‚Üí+*T` with explicit
  `[RingHomCompTriple œÜ œà œá]` (plain `œà.comp œÜ` fails `RingHomCompTriple œÜ œà (œà.comp œÜ)` synth); the
  diamond worry does NOT bite at statement level (`‚Üí‚Çó[R]` defeq `‚Üí‚Çõ‚Çó[RingHom.id R]`); prove via
  `LinearMap.ext_on (ŒπMulti_span / span_tprod_eq_top)` + generator action. (5b) categorical wrapper
  `‚Ä¶baseChangeMapCat : M ‚ü∂ (restrictScalars œÜ).obj N` via `ModuleCat.semilinearMapAddEquiv` ‚Äî this is
  what `mk` consumes (NOT the named `baseChangeMap_{id,comp}`; both share the same generator fact).
  (5c) `PresheafOfModules.{exteriorPower,symPower}` via `PresheafOfModules.mk` (NO smart ctor):
  `obj U := (F.obj U).{ext,sym}Power n` (`letI := X.ringCatSheaf_commRing U`),
  `map f := baseChangeMapCat ((X.ringCatSheaf.presheaf.map f).hom) n (F.map f)`; coherence factored into
  PRIVATE `{ext,sym}PowMap_{id,comp}` (`restrictScalars{Id',Comp'}` form) ‚Äî `mk` accepts the explicit
  by-`rw` proofs via proof-irrelevance on `hf`/`hgf`. TRAPS: `rw [F.map_id/comp]` forces a
  structure-sheaf `whnf` ‚Üí finite but needs `maxHeartbeats 2M`(id)/`4M`(comp); the `_comp` final
  generator rewrite needs `erw` not `rw` (`ConcreteCategory.hom` vs `ModuleCat.Hom.hom` head mismatch,
  defeq not syntactic ‚Äî an F-free helper was abandoned over it). (6)
  `Scheme.Modules.{exteriorPower,symPower}` = `PresheafOfModules.sheafification (ùüô X.ringCatSheaf.obj)`
  applied to `PresheafOfModules.*Power n ((SheafOfModules.forget ‚Ä¶).obj F)` ‚Äî forget base defeq presheaf
  base (no coercion); opens-site sheafification instances auto-resolve via the heavy bridge's
  `sheafificationAdjunction (ùüô R.obj)`. `mathlib:sheafification_modules_exact = PresheafOfModules.sheafification`.
  These are object-level defs, not `X.Modules ‚•§ X.Modules` functors (package later if downstream needs).
- **Eval-map / kernel-bundle substrate (LANDED iter-014, axiom-clean).** `H‚Å∞(F)‚äóO_X‚ÜíF` and
  `M_F` on an arbitrary `X.Modules`: (1) `trivialBundle I := SheafOfModules.free (R := X.ringCatSheaf) I`
  (index-type, no basis/`choose`). (2) `evalMap F := (SheafOfModules.freeHomEquiv F (I := F.sections)).symm id`
  ‚Äî the counit (identity family of sections); index by `F.sections` (`: Type u`), NOT the derived/Ab Œì,
  so universes align and domain is defeq `trivialBundle F.sections`. (3) `evalKernel F := Limits.kernel (evalMap F)`
  ‚Äî `HasKernel` is FREE via the heavy Grothendieck-abelian bridge (no extra instance work). All three
  axiom-clean. `kernelBundle := evalKernel L` is the Basic.lean specialization.
- **Local freeness of the trivial bundle + iso-invariance (LANDED iter-015, axiom-clean).**
  `trivialBundle_isLocallyFree (I : Type u) [Finite I]`: for an open immersion `f`, restriction is
  iso to pullback (`restrictFunctorIsoPullback f : restrictFunctor f ‚âÖ Scheme.Modules.pullback f`),
  whose site functor `Opens.map f.base` is the RIGHT adjoint of the image functor `f.opensFunctor`
  (`f.isOpenEmbedding.isOpenMap.adjunction`) ‚áí FINAL (`Functor.final_of_adjunction`) ‚áí satisfies the
  hypothesis of `SheafOfModules.pullbackObjFreeIso : (pullback œÜ).obj (free I) ‚âÖ free I`. Compose into
  `restrictFunctorObjFreeIso`, then take the single `‚ä§`-cover for every point. The `mapFreeIso`/
  `PreservesColimitsOfShape` route is NOT needed (and the iter-014 "dead end" diagnosis was wrong ‚Äî a
  failed `PreservesColimitsOfShape` synth ‚âÝ a dead route; check adjointness first). `isLocallyFree_of_iso
  (e : F ‚âÖ G) : F.IsLocallyFree ‚Üí G.IsLocallyFree` transports trivialisations across
  `(restrictFunctor U.Œπ).mapIso e` ‚Äî reuse in rank/assembly steps.
- **`Mono` does not propagate through `ShortComplex.mk`.** When building a `ShortComplex.ShortExact`
  whose `f` is a `kernel.Œπ`, `mono_f := inferInstance` FAILS (`failed to synthesize Mono F.‚Ä¶f`); unfold
  the wrapper first: `mono_f := by dsimp only [yourShortComplexDef, ShortComplex.mk]; infer_instance`.
  (`exact := ShortComplex.exact_kernel g`, `epi_g := <epi hyp>`.) Modelling note: there is no
  `GloballyGenerated` predicate on `X.Modules` ‚Äî take `Epi (evalMap F)` as the working hypothesis.
- **Foundation-first scaffold**: realize base objects before dependent statements, so no
  premature untypeable statement stubs accumulate on the DAG frontier.
- **Scaffold/build objective format that PARSES**: in PROGRESS `## Current Objectives`,
  the backtick `.lean` filename must LEAD the numbered bold (`N. **`path.lean`** ‚Äî ‚Ä¶`),
  keyword on the SAME physical line, NO colon before the filename. Two gates
  (`_OBJECTIVE_NUMBERED` regex + `sorry_count` no-op filter) both must pass. A
  zero-open-sorry file (only `opaque` stubs) is ALSO dropped by the no-op filter unless a
  scaffold/build keyword shares the filename's line ‚Äî this silently dropped the
  Foundations build objective in iters 001‚Äì004.

### Conventions / environment
- **`Ab = AddCommGrpCat`** in this mathlib (renamed); foundational cohomology lands in
  `AddCommGrpCat`. **`SheafOfModules.free` needs explicit `(R := ‚Ä¶)` ring sheaf** (e.g.
  `U.toScheme.ringCatSheaf` for an open subscheme).
- **`open AlgebraicGeometry` shadows `evaluation`** ‚Üí the residue-field functor; write
  `CategoryTheory.evaluation` for the functor-category evaluation.
- **Toolchain‚Üîmathlib pin must agree**: `lean-toolchain` and the `lakefile.toml` mathlib
  rev MUST match (project uses `leanprover/lean4:v4.30.0` ‚áî mathlib `v4.30.0`). Mismatch
  ‚áí `Cache/IO.lean` build failure (`Lake.Glob.ofString?` not found) ‚áí `lake exe cache get`
  can't run. Also keep `require mathlib` LAST in `lakefile.toml` so `doc-gen4`/`Cli` don't
  shadow mathlib's `Cli` rev and zero out the cache. `lake update` transiently rewrites
  `lean-toolchain` ‚Äî restore it to `v4.30.0` afterward. See memory `build-toolchain-cache-fix`.
- **Foundations.lean is REAL now** (iter-005): the 6 ex-`opaque` peer stubs there are
  replaced by genuine axiom-clean Mathlib-backed decls. The old "peer anchors are bare
  opaque, never proved" convention is RETIRED for Foundations.lean. (Basic.lean still
  holds 3 intentional `opaque := Unit/True` scaffold stubs ‚Äî `KernelBundleReduction`,
  `UniversalZeroLocus`, `UniversalSecantBundles` ‚Äî replace before they are cited downstream.)
- Ref TeX has invalid-UTF-8 bytes ‚Äî use `LC_ALL=C grep`.
- `archon-lean-lsp` MCP was flaky (diagnostics empty / `lean_run_code` broken pipe /
  "no valid Lean project path"); `lake build <module>` is the authoritative compile check.

### Known Blockers (do not retry as proof lanes)
- **~~`instIsGrothendieckAbelianModules` / `instHasInjectiveResolutionsModules`~~ ‚Äî RESOLVED
  iter-009.** The heavy `R^if_*` bridge LANDED axiom-clean (see the 5-step pattern above). Both
  `AB5OfSize (SheafOfModules R)` and `HasSeparator (SheafOfModules R)` are now proved in
  `Foundations.lean` (built over Mathlib, no contribution needed). `X.Modules` is unconditionally
  Grothendieck abelian + has injective resolutions; `higherDirectImage` self-discharges. Dead end
  recorded: `SheafOfModules.free` / `GeneratingSections` as a separating family ‚Äî sees only GLOBAL
  sections (`Œì` not conservative on schemes); use sheafified `freeYoneda` instead.
- **`Scheme.Modules.exteriorPower` / `symPower`**: genuine multi-lemma gap (iter-006 verified).
  **Module-level `Sym^i` functor RESOLVED iter-010** (`ModuleCat.symmetricPower.functor`,
  axiom-clean ‚Äî see proof pattern above); exterior module-level functor is Mathlib's
  `ModuleCat.exteriorPower.functor`. The REMAINING obstruction for BOTH is the presheaf‚Üísheaf lift:
  (1) `SheafOfModules`/`PresheafOfModules` is `RingCat`-valued ‚Äî `CommRing (X.ringCatSheaf.val.obj U)`
  does NOT synthesize (`found:ringcatsheaf_commring`, effort 600); (2) the openwise functor must be
  base-change functorial over the varying semilinear ring map `R(U)‚ÜíR(V)` ‚Äî NO base-change lemma in
  Mathlib for either power (iter-010 confirmed the instance-plumbing trap, see pattern above;
  `found:{exterior,presheafmod_sym}_power_base_change`). Effort-break before any prover lane; carry
  `Module A N`+`IsScalarTower` explicit or work over `restrictScalars œÜ`. NEVER a thin one-shot port.
  **UPDATE iter-012: obstructions (1) `ringCatSheaf_commring` and (2) base-change `baseChangeMap`
  (both flavours) RESOLVED axiom-clean** (see "Base-change power maps" pattern above). The ONLY
  remaining gap for the openwise/oxmod powers is the `PresheafOfModules.mk` assembly: build
  `baseChangeMap_id` + `baseChangeMap_comp` (in the `restrictScalars{Id',Comp'}` coherence form), then
  `mk`, then sheafify ‚Äî a clean frontier unit, NOT a blocker. No `CorePresheafOfModules` smart
  constructor exists; use generator-`ext` (`map_apply_ŒπMulti` + `baseChangeMap_apply_ŒπMulti`).
  **UPDATE iter-013: FULLY RESOLVED axiom-clean** ‚Äî `PresheafOfModules.{exteriorPower,symPower}` +
  `Scheme.Modules.{exteriorPower,symPower}` both landed (see "Openwise + sheafified power functors"
  pattern above). The entire ‚àß^i/Sym^i-on-`X.Modules` substrate is COMPLETE; this is no longer a blocker.
- **~~trivialBundle local-freeness via `restrictFunctor` ‚Äî DEAD END~~ ‚Äî RESOLVED iter-015 (the
  iter-014 note was a MISDIAGNOSIS).** `trivialBundle_isLocallyFree` LANDED axiom-clean. The iter-014
  claim that `PreservesColimitsOfShape (Discrete I) (restrictFunctor f)` "won't synth at `Type u`" and
  that `pullbackObjFreeIso` is "the wrong functor" were BOTH false. Route that worked:
  `restrictFunctor f ‚âÖ Scheme.Modules.pullback f` (`restrictFunctorIsoPullback`); the pullback's site
  functor is `Opens.map f.base` = the **right** adjoint of the image functor `f.opensFunctor` for an
  open immersion (`f.isOpenEmbedding.isOpenMap.adjunction`), hence **final**
  (`Functor.final_of_adjunction`) ‚Äî exactly the hypothesis of `SheafOfModules.pullbackObjFreeIso`.
  Compose ‚áí `restrictFunctorObjFreeIso : (restrictFunctor f).obj (free I) ‚âÖ free I`; single `‚ä§`-cover
  ‚áí local freeness. The earlier memory conflated **preimage** `Opens.map` (right adjoint ‚áí final) with
  **image** `opensFunctor` (not final). LESSON: a failed `PreservesColimitsOfShape` synth does NOT
  prove a route dead ‚Äî check left/right-adjointness first. Memory corrected:
  `lean-restrictfunctor-free-universe-deadend`.
- **`isLocallyFree_kernel_of_shortExact` (Hartshorne III.6.5) ‚Äî no Mathlib anchor (iter-015).** This is
  now the SOLE real wall for `M_F is locally free` (its siblings `trivial_bundle_locally_free` +
  `eval_shortexact_of_globally_generated` landed iter-015; the eval SES uses hypothesis `Epi (evalMap F)`
  as the working def of "globally generated" ‚Äî no `GloballyGenerated` predicate exists). III.6.5 yields
  freeness only at each **stalk** `ùí¶_x` (SES of `O_{X,x}`-modules, free middle + projective quotient ‚áí
  splits); the project `IsLocallyFree` demands **open** trivialisations of constant rank. The
  **stalk-free ‚áí open-free** bridge (Nakayama / det-non-vanishing + finite presentation) has ZERO
  Mathlib infrastructure (grep-confirmed: no locally-free-sheaf / `VectorBundle` on schemes). Build the
  stalk substrate first (stalk functor `X.Modules‚ÜíModuleCat O_{X,x}`; SES-with-projective-quotient-splits
  via `Module.Projective`+`ShortComplex.Splitting`; stalk‚Üíopen bridge for f.p. modules) ‚Äî each its own
  mathlib-build lane. effort-break BEFORE any prover lane; do NOT assign as a one-shot.
- **`rank_kernel_of_shortExact`, `evalKernel_isLocallyFree` ‚Äî blocked on Core 2 above (iter-015).** Both
  are pure assembly atop `isLocallyFree_kernel_of_shortExact`; assigning either before it forces a fake
  hypothesis-conditional stub. They unblock automatically once Core 2 lands.
- **Headline statements + USB sec-1 lemma stubs (Basic.lean)**: cannot be typed until the
  base geometry `Type` defs are fleshed beyond `:= Unit`. `K_{p,q}` now HAS a blueprint
  pin (`MR4213770.koszulCohomology`, USB ch. L9) ‚Äî the iter-003 "no pin" blocker is
  resolved; the twist (‚äóL) convention is a Lean-lane consistency check when
  `kernelBundle`/`koszulCohomology` are built.

## Last Updated
2026-06-19T (iter-015 review)
